title: Define a precise contract per stage
description: Give each pipeline stage an explicit input/output invariant so bugs are localized and stages can be tested in isolation.
body: Use when structuring the compiler pipeline (lex, parse, typecheck, IR, optimize, codegen). Do not let stages reach across boundaries or assume undocumented invariants about each other's output. Actions: specify what each stage requires and guarantees (e.g. parser yields a well-formed AST, type checker annotates every node, IR is in SSA), add a verifier that checks the invariant between stages, and test each stage against its contract. Validation signals include the inter-stage verifier passing and unit tests per stage. Risk boundary: a violated cross-stage invariant surfaces as a confusing failure far downstream; treat verifier failures as blocking and fix the producing stage rather than patching the consumer.
